articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Green Hydrogen Incentive Package to Boost Domestic Production
articles.explainer:
• Government announces a comprehensive incentive scheme for Green Hydrogen production and adoption, including Production-Linked Incentives (PLI).
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen manufacturing and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Policy includes demand creation mechanisms, R&D support, and a framework for green hydrogen ecosystem development.
• Targets significant reduction in carbon emissions, energy import bill, and creation of new green jobs by 2030.
• Encourages private sector investment and technological innovation across the value chain, from production to end-use.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
The incentive package includes Production-Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Targets include reducing fossil fuel imports and achieving climate goals by 2030.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to leverage green hydrogen for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the economic implications of the Green Hydrogen Mission on industrial growth, job creation, and export potential.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine how the PLI scheme can accelerate indigenous manufacturing and technological advancements in the green hydrogen sector.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Verdict on Petitions Challenging Electoral Bonds Scheme
articles.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution bench of the Supreme Court concludes hearings on a batch of petitions challenging the legality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Arguments centered on transparency in political funding, citizens' right to information, and potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• Petitioners argued the scheme promotes opacity and violates Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution.
• The government defended the scheme as a step towards cleaner political funding and protecting donor anonymity.
• The verdict, expected in early 2026, could significantly reshape election finance regulations in India.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017 through Finance Act.
The scheme allows for anonymous donations to political parties.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of violating Article 19(1)(a) (freedom of speech and expression, including right to information).
The matter is heard by a Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of transparency in political funding and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values related to electoral processes and citizens' rights.
Analyze the impact of electoral finance regulations on the fairness and integrity of elections in India.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel on legal books in a courtroom setting, symbolizing judiciary and law.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in India in 1951-52 were conducted over four months.

articles.headline: India Concludes Successful Central Asia Dialogue, Emphasizing Regional Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The 7th India-Central Asia Dialogue concludes in New Delhi, bringing together Foreign Ministers from both sides.
• Key focus areas included enhancing trade, investment, and strengthening regional connectivity, particularly through the Chabahar Port and INSTC.
• Discussions also covered cooperation on regional security, counter-terrorism efforts, and the evolving situation in Afghanistan.
• Ministers agreed on joint projects in various sectors such as energy, information technology, and healthcare.
• India reiterated its commitment to its 'Connect Central Asia' policy, aiming for deeper strategic and economic partnerships.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Dialogue is a ministerial-level forum.
Chabahar Port is located in Iran and is crucial for India's connectivity to Central Asia.
INSTC (International North-South Transport Corridor) is a multi-modal network for freight movement.
Central Asian countries include Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic ambitions.
Discuss how enhanced connectivity projects like Chabahar Port and INSTC can transform India's trade relations with Central Asia.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in India's efforts to strengthen regional security cooperation with Central Asian nations.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries, highlighting trade routes like INSTC.
articles.trivia: Central Asia was historically part of the Silk Road, connecting East and West.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focus on Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decides to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision is primarily driven by moderating retail inflation figures and a desire to support ongoing economic growth momentum.
• RBI Governor emphasizes continued vigilance on global economic uncertainties and potential risks to inflation from crude oil prices.
• The MPC projects robust GDP growth for the current fiscal year, signaling confidence in India's economic resilience.
• Focus remains on ensuring financial stability and anchoring inflation expectations within the target range.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy repo rate.
The MPC consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by GoI).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the trade-offs between controlling inflation and promoting economic growth in India's monetary policy framework.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the MPC in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and growth.
articles.imageDescription: RBI emblem and a graph showing economic indicators like inflation and interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Gaganyaan Mission Crew Escape System
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully completes a crucial abort mission test for the Gaganyaan program.
• The test validates the efficacy of the crew escape system, designed to safely pull astronauts away from the launch vehicle in case of an emergency.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's preparations for its first human spaceflight mission.
• Further tests on parachute deployment systems and re-entry modules are scheduled in the coming months.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit by 2026, showcasing indigenous space capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The recent test focused on the Crew Escape System (CES).
CES is designed to ensure astronaut safety during launch anomalies.
ISRO is the agency responsible for the Gaganyaan program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its geopolitical implications.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight and ISRO's indigenous capabilities to overcome them.
Examine the potential spin-off benefits of the Gaganyaan mission for other sectors of the Indian economy and scientific research.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module separating from a rocket during an abort test.
articles.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11.

articles.headline: Delhi's Air Quality Shows Marginal Improvement Amidst Stricter Enforcement of Anti-Pollution Measures
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) indicates a slight reduction in average PM2.5 levels in Delhi compared to previous winter seasons.
• This marginal improvement is attributed to stricter implementation of the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) and sustained efforts to curb stubble burning.
• Despite progress, challenges persist with vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and construction dust contributing significantly to poor air quality.
• Environmental experts call for long-term, regional strategies and greater public participation for sustainable air quality improvement.
• Government emphasizes accelerated transition to cleaner fuels, electric vehicles, and green infrastructure projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: CPCB is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
GRAP (Graded Response Action Plan) is a set of emergency measures to tackle air pollution.
PM2.5 refers to particulate matter smaller than 2.5 micrometers, a major air pollutant.
Stubble burning in neighboring states is a significant contributor to Delhi's winter pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Delhi and the NCR region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like GRAP and efforts to curb stubble burning.
Suggest comprehensive long-term strategies, including technological solutions and policy interventions, to ensure sustainable air quality improvement.
articles.imageDescription: Skyline of Delhi shrouded in smog, with people wearing masks.
articles.trivia: The first major smog event recorded in history was in London in December 1952, known as 'The Great Smog'.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education holds a high-level meeting to assess the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Significant progress is noted in curriculum reforms, foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) initiatives, and digital education platforms.
• Challenges highlighted include varying state-level adoption rates, infrastructure development gaps, and the need for extensive teacher training.
• Emphasis is placed on continuous professional development for educators and fostering multidisciplinary learning environments.
• The long-term goal is to ensure equitable access to quality education and prepare students for 21st-century skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system by 2040.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focuses on foundational literacy and numeracy as a national mission.
Promotes multidisciplinary learning and vocational education from early stages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020.
Analyze the challenges faced in the effective implementation of NEP 2020 across diverse states and regions in India.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education as envisioned by the policy.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was a landmark commission that recommended a 10+2+3 pattern for education in India.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework with New Guidelines for Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator's office releases updated guidelines aimed at protecting Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) in India.
• The new framework focuses on enhancing resilience against sophisticated cyber threats, including state-sponsored attacks and ransomware.
• Mandates regular security audits, deployment of advanced threat detection systems, and robust incident response protocols for CII entities.
• Encourages greater public-private partnerships for intelligence sharing, capacity building, and collaborative cyber defense.
• Aims to safeguard essential services such as power grids, banking, telecommunications, and transport networks from cyber disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
prelimsPoints: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy.
National Cyber Security Coordinator advises the Prime Minister on cyber security issues.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
New guidelines aim to enhance resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing importance of cyber security in protecting India's critical infrastructure in the digital age.
Analyze the key provisions of the new cyber security guidelines and their potential impact on national security.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cyber security framework, including technological, human resource, and legal aspects.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a network of interconnected servers, symbolizing cyber security.
articles.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Elk Cloner', appeared in 1982, targeting Apple II systems.

articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations See Further Progress, Focus on Key Divergence Areas
articles.explainer:
• The latest round of negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) concludes with renewed optimism from both sides.
• Discussions focused on resolving key divergence areas, including intellectual property rights, tariffs on sensitive products like automobiles, and market access for services.
• Both India and the EU express strong political commitment to concluding the comprehensive agreement for enhanced trade and investment flows.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost India's exports to the EU and facilitate technology transfer and foreign direct investment.
• Challenges remain in areas of non-tariff barriers, sustainable development provisions, and digital trade rules.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and the EU are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
Key contentious issues often include tariffs, IPR, and market access for services.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and strategic importance of a comprehensive India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Discuss the major sticking points in the India-EU FTA negotiations and how they can be resolved.
Evaluate the impact of such an agreement on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, services, and agriculture.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a graph showing trade growth.
articles.trivia: The European Union is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.

articles.headline: New Tiger Reserve Proposed in Western Ghats to Bolster Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has initiated a proposal for establishing a new tiger reserve in the Western Ghats.
• The proposed site is located in a critical biodiversity hotspot, aiming to provide additional protected habitat for the growing tiger population.
• This initiative seeks to strengthen Project Tiger and conserve a wide array of endemic species found in the region.
• The proposal emphasizes local community participation for sustainable management, anti-poaching measures, and human-wildlife conflict mitigation.
• Highlights India's continuous commitment to biodiversity conservation and achieving its tiger population recovery goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers in India.
Tiger reserves are legally protected areas for tiger conservation.
MoEFCC is the nodal ministry for wildlife conservation in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the challenges it faces from human activities.
Analyze the role of Project Tiger in India's conservation success and the need for expanding protected areas.
Examine the importance of community participation and sustainable management practices in wildlife conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A Bengal tiger walking through dense forest in the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme Boosts Domestic Manufacturing Across Key Sectors
articles.explainer:
• A recent government report highlights significant investment and production growth across various sectors benefiting from the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes.
• Sectors such as electronics, pharmaceuticals, textiles, and automotive components have shown robust performance and increased capacity utilization.
• The PLI scheme aims to make India a global manufacturing hub, reduce import dependence, and integrate into global supply chains.
• Key outcomes include the creation of new employment opportunities and enhancement of India's export competitiveness.
• Discussions are ongoing to potentially expand the PLI scheme to include new 'sunrise sectors' and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: PLI schemes offer incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
The scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and attract foreign investment.
It covers multiple sectors like electronics, pharma, automobiles, textiles, etc.
Part of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and outcomes of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in fostering domestic manufacturing and economic growth.
Discuss how the PLI scheme contributes to India's goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub and reducing import dependence.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with expanding the PLI scheme to new and emerging sectors.
articles.imageDescription: Factory assembly line with robotic arms, symbolizing modern manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The 'Make in India' initiative was launched in 2014 to encourage companies to manufacture in India.

articles.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Discusses Reforms in Centre-State Fiscal Relations
articles.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convenes to address critical issues concerning fiscal devolution and resource sharing between the Centre and States.
• Several states advocate for greater financial autonomy and an increased share in central tax revenues to meet their developmental needs.
• Discussions include the future of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation mechanism post-2026 and its implications for state finances.
• The Council emphasizes the spirit of cooperative federalism as essential for achieving national development goals.
• Recommendations from the meeting are expected to provide crucial inputs to the upcoming 16th Finance Commission's mandate.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Council is a constitutional body established under Article 263.
Its mandate is to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Centre and states.
The Council is chaired by the Prime Minister.
GST compensation mechanism was put in place for five years (2017-2022) but extended for some cases, now under review for post-2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Inter-State Council in promoting cooperative federalism in India.
Analyze the challenges in Centre-State fiscal relations, particularly concerning resource sharing and GST compensation.
Suggest reforms to strengthen fiscal federalism and ensure greater financial autonomy for states while maintaining national cohesion.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolizing legislative and federal discussions.
articles.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission (1983) recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.

articles.headline: Indian Researchers Achieve Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Algorithm Development
articles.explainer:
• A collaborative team of scientists from IIT Delhi and the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR) announces a significant advance in quantum algorithm design.
• The newly developed algorithm promises faster and more efficient solutions for complex computational problems, outperforming classical algorithms in specific scenarios.
• Potential applications span various fields, including drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, and advanced cryptography.
• This breakthrough highlights India's growing capabilities and expertise in the rapidly evolving field of quantum technology.
• The government's National Quantum Mission continues to provide crucial funding and infrastructure support for cutting-edge research.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to solve complex problems.
Qubits are the basic units of information in quantum computing.
National Quantum Mission was launched by the Indian government to promote quantum technology.
Applications include drug discovery, materials science, and cryptography.
mainsPoints: Discuss the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize various sectors.
Analyze India's efforts and achievements in quantum technology under the National Quantum Mission.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing, particularly concerning cryptography.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum bits (qubits) forming a network.
articles.trivia: IBM launched the world's first integrated quantum computing system for commercial use in 2019.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Skill Development Initiative for Women in Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development introduces a new flagship scheme aimed at empowering rural women through comprehensive skill development programs.
• The initiative focuses on providing vocational skills relevant to local economies, digital literacy, and entrepreneurship training.
• Aims to enhance women's participation in the formal workforce, promote financial independence, and foster micro-enterprise development.
• Partnerships with Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are crucial for effective outreach and grassroots implementation.
• Part of broader government efforts to achieve gender equality, inclusive growth, and uplift vulnerable sections of society.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: The scheme targets women in rural areas for skill development.
Focuses on vocational skills, digital literacy, and entrepreneurship.
NGOs and SHGs play a crucial role in implementation.
Aims to increase women's workforce participation and financial independence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in promoting women's empowerment and economic independence in rural India.
Analyze the role of government schemes and civil society organizations (NGOs, SHGs) in fostering women's entrepreneurship.
Examine the challenges faced by rural women in accessing skill training and productive employment opportunities, and suggest policy interventions.
articles.imageDescription: Group of rural women participating in a skill training workshop.
articles.trivia: India's National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 to promote skill development.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
articles.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations addresses the General Assembly, strongly advocating for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council.
• Emphasizes the critical need for an expanded Security Council that truly reflects contemporary global realities, not post-WWII power structures.
• Advocates for increased representation for developing countries, including permanent membership for India, in a reformed Council.
• Highlights the urgency of reforms to make the UN more effective, legitimate, and responsive to current global challenges.
• Calls for a time-bound and transparent process to achieve meaningful changes in the Council's composition and working methods.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India has consistently sought permanent membership in the UNSC.
The P5 members have veto power.
UN reforms aim to make the organization more representative and effective.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's persistent demand for permanent membership in the UN Security Council and the justifications behind it.
Discuss the challenges and complexities involved in achieving comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council.
Examine how a reformed and expanded UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges and reflect multilateralism.
articles.imageDescription: United Nations headquarters building in New York, with flags of member states.
articles.trivia: The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945, in San Francisco, at the conclusion of the United Nations Conference on International Organization.

articles.headline: New Solar Power Project Inaugurated in Ladakh, Boosting Regional Energy Security
articles.explainer:
• A large-scale solar power project is inaugurated in Ladakh, marking a significant step towards the region's energy independence.
• The project leverages Ladakh's high solar insolation to generate clean energy, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and grid power.
• It aims to provide stable and reliable electricity to remote areas, addressing long-standing energy deficits and developmental needs.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to develop renewable energy infrastructure in border regions and promote sustainable development.
• Expected to foster local employment, reduce carbon footprint, and contribute to India's climate action goals.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Ladakh has high potential for solar energy due to its high altitude and clear skies.
Solar power projects contribute to India's renewable energy targets.
Such projects help reduce reliance on conventional energy sources in remote areas.
Part of India's commitment to climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing renewable energy infrastructure in regions like Ladakh.
Analyze the environmental and socio-economic benefits of large-scale solar projects in mountainous terrains.
Examine the challenges involved in implementing and maintaining renewable energy projects in remote, high-altitude areas.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels installed in a mountainous landscape of Ladakh under clear skies.
articles.trivia: India is the world's third-largest producer of renewable energy.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tourism Unveils New Policy to Promote Sustainable and Responsible Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launches a comprehensive new policy aimed at fostering sustainable and responsible tourism practices across India.
• Focuses on promoting eco-tourism, cultural heritage tourism, and rural tourism while minimizing environmental impact.
• Emphasizes community involvement, local employment generation, and preservation of indigenous cultures and traditions.
• Includes provisions for digital promotion, infrastructure development, and skill enhancement for tourism sector professionals.
• Seeks to position India as a global leader in sustainable tourism, attracting mindful travelers and ensuring long-term industry growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: The new policy aims for sustainable and responsible tourism.
Focuses on eco-tourism, cultural heritage, and rural tourism.
Emphasizes community involvement and local employment.
Ministry of Tourism is the nodal body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of sustainable tourism and its importance for India's diverse cultural and natural heritage.
Analyze how the new tourism policy can promote inclusive growth and empower local communities.
Examine the challenges in implementing responsible tourism practices and suggest measures to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse Indian landscapes, from mountains to beaches, with tourists enjoying responsibly.
articles.trivia: The Taj Mahal, one of India's most famous tourist destinations, attracts millions of visitors annually.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Global Digital Public Infrastructure Framework at G20 Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India advocates for a global framework on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) during a G20 working group meeting.
• Highlights the success of its own DPIs like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker in fostering financial inclusion and digital governance.
• Proposes international cooperation for developing open-source, interoperable DPIs to bridge the global digital divide.
• Emphasizes the need for inclusive, secure, and resilient digital ecosystems accessible to all nations.
• Aims to leverage its G20 presidency experiences to promote a common understanding and adoption of DPI principles worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to digital systems for public good.
Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are examples of India's successful DPIs.
India champions DPI at international forums like G20.
Aims to bridge the global digital divide and promote inclusive digital ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for governance and development.
Analyze India's leadership role in promoting DPI on the global stage, drawing examples from its own experience.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing a global framework for DPI, considering issues of data privacy, security, and interoperability.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing various digital services like payments, identity, and cloud storage.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

highlights: India unveils ambitious Green Hydrogen incentive package to boost domestic production and achieve climate goals.
Supreme Court reserves verdict on petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds scheme, a decision expected to impact political funding transparency.
ISRO successfully conducts a critical abort mission test for the Gaganyaan program's crew escape system, a major step for human spaceflight.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains status quo on repo rate, balancing easing inflation with a focus on supporting economic growth.
India concludes successful Central Asia Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced regional connectivity, trade, and security cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The mission includes Production-Linked Incentives (PLI) for both electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using fossil fuel-based electricity.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Statement 2 is correct: The incentive package includes PLI for both electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production. Statement 3 is incorrect: Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy, not fossil fuel-based electricity. Fossil fuel-based electrolysis would produce 'grey hydrogen'.

prelimsMocks.question: The Supreme Court recently concluded hearings on petitions challenging the Electoral Bonds scheme. The petitioners primarily argued that the scheme violates which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution?
options: (c) Article 21 (Protection of life and personal liberty)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The petitioners challenging the Electoral Bonds scheme primarily argued that it promotes opacity in political funding and hence infringes upon the citizens' right to information, which is considered an integral part of the fundamental right to freedom of speech and expression guaranteed under Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the benchmark policy repo rate in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a statutory and institutionalized framework under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, for maintaining price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. It is entrusted with the task of determining the policy repo rate.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Gaganyaan Mission:
1. It is India's first mission to send humans to the Moon.
2. The recent test conducted by ISRO was for the Crew Escape System.
3. The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission to low Earth orbit, not to the Moon. Statement 2 is correct: ISRO recently conducted a critical abort mission test for the Crew Escape System. Statement 3 is correct: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit.

prelimsMocks.question: The Inter-State Council is a constitutional body established under which Article of the Indian Constitution?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Inter-State Council is a non-permanent constitutional body set up by a presidential order on the basis of provisions in Article 263 of the Indian Constitution. Its mandate is to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Union and the States or among States.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss India's potential to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and its implications for the nation's energy security and climate goals. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy; Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Green Hydrogen Mission, its goals (global hub, energy security, climate). Discuss India's advantages (renewable potential, policy support like PLI). Elaborate on implications: reduced import bill, decarbonization, new industry, job creation. Conclude with challenges and way forward.

mainsMocks.question: Critically examine the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of transparency in political funding and democratic principles. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary; Parliament; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Define Electoral Bonds and their purpose. Arguments FOR: cleaner money, donor anonymity, reduced black money. Arguments AGAINST: opacity, lack of transparency, violation of RTI (Article 19(1)(a)), potential for quid pro quo, disproportionate benefit to ruling party. Discuss Supreme Court's role and the need for balancing transparency with legitimate concerns. Conclude with suggestions for reform.

mainsMocks.question: In light of recent discussions on Centre-State fiscal relations, analyze the challenges and opportunities for strengthening cooperative federalism in India. (200 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Define cooperative federalism and its importance. Challenges: fiscal imbalances, GST compensation issues, state demands for greater financial autonomy, differing priorities. Opportunities: Inter-State Council, Finance Commission's role, collaborative policy-making, shared goals for national development. Suggest measures like greater fiscal devolution, transparent mechanisms, and institutional strengthening.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to achieve a green hydrogen production capacity of 5 MMT per annum by 2030.
Article 263 of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of an Inter-State Council.
The Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate India's human spaceflight capability to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI is headed by the Governor of the Reserve Bank of India.
Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) is a set of emergency measures implemented to combat air pollution in Delhi-NCR.
The Western Ghats are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence.
The right to information is considered an integral part of Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution.
Quantum computing utilizes qubits, which can exist in multiple states simultaneously due to superposition.
The UN Security Council has five permanent members: China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

GS1.headline: Government Launches New Skill Development Initiative for Women in Rural Areas
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development introduces a new flagship scheme aimed at empowering rural women through comprehensive skill development programs.
• The initiative focuses on providing vocational skills relevant to local economies, digital literacy, and entrepreneurship training.
• Aims to enhance women's participation in the formal workforce, promote financial independence, and foster micro-enterprise development.
• Partnerships with Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) and Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are crucial for effective outreach and grassroots implementation.
• Part of broader government efforts to achieve gender equality, inclusive growth, and uplift vulnerable sections of society.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
prelimsPoints: The scheme targets women in rural areas for skill development.
Focuses on vocational skills, digital literacy, and entrepreneurship.
NGOs and SHGs play a crucial role in implementation.
Aims to increase women's workforce participation and financial independence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of skill development in promoting women's empowerment and economic independence in rural India.
Analyze the role of government schemes and civil society organizations (NGOs, SHGs) in fostering women's entrepreneurship.
Examine the challenges faced by rural women in accessing skill training and productive employment opportunities, and suggest policy interventions.
GS1.imageDescription: Group of rural women participating in a skill training workshop.
GS1.trivia: India's National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 to promote skill development.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Tourism Unveils New Policy to Promote Sustainable and Responsible Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism launches a comprehensive new policy aimed at fostering sustainable and responsible tourism practices across India.
• Focuses on promoting eco-tourism, cultural heritage tourism, and rural tourism while minimizing environmental impact.
• Emphasizes community involvement, local employment generation, and preservation of indigenous cultures and traditions.
• Includes provisions for digital promotion, infrastructure development, and skill enhancement for tourism sector professionals.
• Seeks to position India as a global leader in sustainable tourism, attracting mindful travelers and ensuring long-term industry growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: The new policy aims for sustainable and responsible tourism.
Focuses on eco-tourism, cultural heritage, and rural tourism.
Emphasizes community involvement and local employment.
Ministry of Tourism is the nodal body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of sustainable tourism and its importance for India's diverse cultural and natural heritage.
Analyze how the new tourism policy can promote inclusive growth and empower local communities.
Examine the challenges in implementing responsible tourism practices and suggest measures to overcome them.
GS1.imageDescription: Diverse Indian landscapes, from mountains to beaches, with tourists enjoying responsibly.
GS1.trivia: The Taj Mahal, one of India's most famous tourist destinations, attracts millions of visitors annually.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Verdict on Petitions Challenging Electoral Bonds Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution bench of the Supreme Court concludes hearings on a batch of petitions challenging the legality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Arguments centered on transparency in political funding, citizens' right to information, and potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• Petitioners argued the scheme promotes opacity and violates Article 19(1)(a) of the Constitution.
• The government defended the scheme as a step towards cleaner political funding and protecting donor anonymity.
• The verdict, expected in early 2026, could significantly reshape election finance regulations in India.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds scheme was introduced in 2017 through Finance Act.
The scheme allows for anonymous donations to political parties.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of violating Article 19(1)(a) (freedom of speech and expression, including right to information).
The matter is heard by a Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of transparency in political funding and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values related to electoral processes and citizens' rights.
Analyze the impact of electoral finance regulations on the fairness and integrity of elections in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel on legal books in a courtroom setting, symbolizing judiciary and law.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in India in 1951-52 were conducted over four months.

GS2.headline: India Concludes Successful Central Asia Dialogue, Emphasizing Regional Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The 7th India-Central Asia Dialogue concludes in New Delhi, bringing together Foreign Ministers from both sides.
• Key focus areas included enhancing trade, investment, and strengthening regional connectivity, particularly through the Chabahar Port and INSTC.
• Discussions also covered cooperation on regional security, counter-terrorism efforts, and the evolving situation in Afghanistan.
• Ministers agreed on joint projects in various sectors such as energy, information technology, and healthcare.
• India reiterated its commitment to its 'Connect Central Asia' policy, aiming for deeper strategic and economic partnerships.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India-Central Asia Dialogue is a ministerial-level forum.
Chabahar Port is located in Iran and is crucial for India's connectivity to Central Asia.
INSTC (International North-South Transport Corridor) is a multi-modal network for freight movement.
Central Asian countries include Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India's foreign policy and economic ambitions.
Discuss how enhanced connectivity projects like Chabahar Port and INSTC can transform India's trade relations with Central Asia.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in India's efforts to strengthen regional security cooperation with Central Asian nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing India and Central Asian countries, highlighting trade routes like INSTC.
GS2.trivia: Central Asia was historically part of the Silk Road, connecting East and West.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education holds a high-level meeting to assess the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• Significant progress is noted in curriculum reforms, foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) initiatives, and digital education platforms.
• Challenges highlighted include varying state-level adoption rates, infrastructure development gaps, and the need for extensive teacher training.
• Emphasis is placed on continuous professional development for educators and fostering multidisciplinary learning environments.
• The long-term goal is to ensure equitable access to quality education and prepare students for 21st-century skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system by 2040.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
Focuses on foundational literacy and numeracy as a national mission.
Promotes multidisciplinary learning and vocational education from early stages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key features and transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020.
Analyze the challenges faced in the effective implementation of NEP 2020 across diverse states and regions in India.
Suggest measures to overcome implementation hurdles and ensure equitable access to quality education as envisioned by the policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was a landmark commission that recommended a 10+2+3 pattern for education in India.

GS2.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations See Further Progress, Focus on Key Divergence Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The latest round of negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) concludes with renewed optimism from both sides.
• Discussions focused on resolving key divergence areas, including intellectual property rights, tariffs on sensitive products like automobiles, and market access for services.
• Both India and the EU express strong political commitment to concluding the comprehensive agreement for enhanced trade and investment flows.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost India's exports to the EU and facilitate technology transfer and foreign direct investment.
• Challenges remain in areas of non-tariff barriers, sustainable development provisions, and digital trade rules.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India and the EU are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
Key contentious issues often include tariffs, IPR, and market access for services.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
FTAs aim to reduce trade barriers between signatory countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and strategic importance of a comprehensive India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Discuss the major sticking points in the India-EU FTA negotiations and how they can be resolved.
Evaluate the impact of such an agreement on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, services, and agriculture.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a graph showing trade growth.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.

GS2.headline: Inter-State Council Meeting Discusses Reforms in Centre-State Fiscal Relations
GS2.explainer:
• The Inter-State Council convenes to address critical issues concerning fiscal devolution and resource sharing between the Centre and States.
• Several states advocate for greater financial autonomy and an increased share in central tax revenues to meet their developmental needs.
• Discussions include the future of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) compensation mechanism post-2026 and its implications for state finances.
• The Council emphasizes the spirit of cooperative federalism as essential for achieving national development goals.
• Recommendations from the meeting are expected to provide crucial inputs to the upcoming 16th Finance Commission's mandate.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Inter-State Council is a constitutional body established under Article 263.
Its mandate is to investigate and discuss subjects of common interest between the Centre and states.
The Council is chaired by the Prime Minister.
GST compensation mechanism was put in place for five years (2017-2022) but extended for some cases, now under review for post-2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Inter-State Council in promoting cooperative federalism in India.
Analyze the challenges in Centre-State fiscal relations, particularly concerning resource sharing and GST compensation.
Suggest reforms to strengthen fiscal federalism and ensure greater financial autonomy for states while maintaining national cohesion.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building, symbolizing legislative and federal discussions.
GS2.trivia: The Sarkaria Commission (1983) recommended the establishment of a permanent Inter-State Council.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UN Security Council Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations addresses the General Assembly, strongly advocating for comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council.
• Emphasizes the critical need for an expanded Security Council that truly reflects contemporary global realities, not post-WWII power structures.
• Advocates for increased representation for developing countries, including permanent membership for India, in a reformed Council.
• Highlights the urgency of reforms to make the UN more effective, legitimate, and responsive to current global challenges.
• Calls for a time-bound and transparent process to achieve meaningful changes in the Council's composition and working methods.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: UN Security Council has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
India has consistently sought permanent membership in the UNSC.
The P5 members have veto power.
UN reforms aim to make the organization more representative and effective.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's persistent demand for permanent membership in the UN Security Council and the justifications behind it.
Discuss the challenges and complexities involved in achieving comprehensive reforms of the UN Security Council.
Examine how a reformed and expanded UNSC could better address contemporary global challenges and reflect multilateralism.
GS2.imageDescription: United Nations headquarters building in New York, with flags of member states.
GS2.trivia: The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945, in San Francisco, at the conclusion of the United Nations Conference on International Organization.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Global Digital Public Infrastructure Framework at G20 Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India advocates for a global framework on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) during a G20 working group meeting.
• Highlights the success of its own DPIs like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker in fostering financial inclusion and digital governance.
• Proposes international cooperation for developing open-source, interoperable DPIs to bridge the global digital divide.
• Emphasizes the need for inclusive, secure, and resilient digital ecosystems accessible to all nations.
• Aims to leverage its G20 presidency experiences to promote a common understanding and adoption of DPI principles worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to digital systems for public good.
Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are examples of India's successful DPIs.
India champions DPI at international forums like G20.
Aims to bridge the global digital divide and promote inclusive digital ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for governance and development.
Analyze India's leadership role in promoting DPI on the global stage, drawing examples from its own experience.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing a global framework for DPI, considering issues of data privacy, security, and interoperability.
GS2.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing various digital services like payments, identity, and cloud storage.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Green Hydrogen Incentive Package to Boost Domestic Production
GS3.explainer:
• Government announces a comprehensive incentive scheme for Green Hydrogen production and adoption, including Production-Linked Incentives (PLI).
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen manufacturing and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Policy includes demand creation mechanisms, R&D support, and a framework for green hydrogen ecosystem development.
• Targets significant reduction in carbon emissions, energy import bill, and creation of new green jobs by 2030.
• Encourages private sector investment and technological innovation across the value chain, from production to end-use.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen.
The incentive package includes Production-Linked Incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Targets include reducing fossil fuel imports and achieving climate goals by 2030.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategy to leverage green hydrogen for energy security and climate change mitigation.
Analyze the economic implications of the Green Hydrogen Mission on industrial growth, job creation, and export potential.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine how the PLI scheme can accelerate indigenous manufacturing and technological advancements in the green hydrogen sector.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focus on Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decides to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision is primarily driven by moderating retail inflation figures and a desire to support ongoing economic growth momentum.
• RBI Governor emphasizes continued vigilance on global economic uncertainties and potential risks to inflation from crude oil prices.
• The MPC projects robust GDP growth for the current fiscal year, signaling confidence in India's economic resilience.
• Focus remains on ensuring financial stability and anchoring inflation expectations within the target range.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy repo rate.
The MPC consists of 6 members (3 from RBI, 3 appointed by GoI).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the trade-offs between controlling inflation and promoting economic growth in India's monetary policy framework.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the MPC in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and growth.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI emblem and a graph showing economic indicators like inflation and interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Gaganyaan Mission Crew Escape System
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully completes a crucial abort mission test for the Gaganyaan program.
• The test validates the efficacy of the crew escape system, designed to safely pull astronauts away from the launch vehicle in case of an emergency.
• This marks a significant milestone in India's preparations for its first human spaceflight mission.
• Further tests on parachute deployment systems and re-entry modules are scheduled in the coming months.
• The Gaganyaan mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit by 2026, showcasing indigenous space capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The recent test focused on the Crew Escape System (CES).
CES is designed to ensure astronaut safety during launch anomalies.
ISRO is the agency responsible for the Gaganyaan program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its geopolitical implications.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight and ISRO's indigenous capabilities to overcome them.
Examine the potential spin-off benefits of the Gaganyaan mission for other sectors of the Indian economy and scientific research.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module separating from a rocket during an abort test.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11.

GS3.headline: Delhi's Air Quality Shows Marginal Improvement Amidst Stricter Enforcement of Anti-Pollution Measures
GS3.explainer:
• A report by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) indicates a slight reduction in average PM2.5 levels in Delhi compared to previous winter seasons.
• This marginal improvement is attributed to stricter implementation of the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) and sustained efforts to curb stubble burning.
• Despite progress, challenges persist with vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, and construction dust contributing significantly to poor air quality.
• Environmental experts call for long-term, regional strategies and greater public participation for sustainable air quality improvement.
• Government emphasizes accelerated transition to cleaner fuels, electric vehicles, and green infrastructure projects.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: CPCB is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
GRAP (Graded Response Action Plan) is a set of emergency measures to tackle air pollution.
PM2.5 refers to particulate matter smaller than 2.5 micrometers, a major air pollutant.
Stubble burning in neighboring states is a significant contributor to Delhi's winter pollution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted causes of air pollution in Delhi and the NCR region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like GRAP and efforts to curb stubble burning.
Suggest comprehensive long-term strategies, including technological solutions and policy interventions, to ensure sustainable air quality improvement.
GS3.imageDescription: Skyline of Delhi shrouded in smog, with people wearing masks.
GS3.trivia: The first major smog event recorded in history was in London in December 1952, known as 'The Great Smog'.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Cyber Security Framework with New Guidelines for Critical Infrastructure Protection
GS3.explainer:
• The National Cyber Security Coordinator's office releases updated guidelines aimed at protecting Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) in India.
• The new framework focuses on enhancing resilience against sophisticated cyber threats, including state-sponsored attacks and ransomware.
• Mandates regular security audits, deployment of advanced threat detection systems, and robust incident response protocols for CII entities.
• Encourages greater public-private partnerships for intelligence sharing, capacity building, and collaborative cyber defense.
• Aims to safeguard essential services such as power grids, banking, telecommunications, and transport networks from cyber disruptions.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
prelimsPoints: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) refers to assets vital for national security and economy.
National Cyber Security Coordinator advises the Prime Minister on cyber security issues.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
New guidelines aim to enhance resilience against sophisticated cyber threats.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing importance of cyber security in protecting India's critical infrastructure in the digital age.
Analyze the key provisions of the new cyber security guidelines and their potential impact on national security.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cyber security framework, including technological, human resource, and legal aspects.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a network of interconnected servers, symbolizing cyber security.
GS3.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Elk Cloner', appeared in 1982, targeting Apple II systems.

GS3.headline: New Tiger Reserve Proposed in Western Ghats to Bolster Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) has initiated a proposal for establishing a new tiger reserve in the Western Ghats.
• The proposed site is located in a critical biodiversity hotspot, aiming to provide additional protected habitat for the growing tiger population.
• This initiative seeks to strengthen Project Tiger and conserve a wide array of endemic species found in the region.
• The proposal emphasizes local community participation for sustainable management, anti-poaching measures, and human-wildlife conflict mitigation.
• Highlights India's continuous commitment to biodiversity conservation and achieving its tiger population recovery goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers in India.
Tiger reserves are legally protected areas for tiger conservation.
MoEFCC is the nodal ministry for wildlife conservation in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the challenges it faces from human activities.
Analyze the role of Project Tiger in India's conservation success and the need for expanding protected areas.
Examine the importance of community participation and sustainable management practices in wildlife conservation efforts.
GS3.imageDescription: A Bengal tiger walking through dense forest in the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's tiger population.

GS3.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme Boosts Domestic Manufacturing Across Key Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• A recent government report highlights significant investment and production growth across various sectors benefiting from the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes.
• Sectors such as electronics, pharmaceuticals, textiles, and automotive components have shown robust performance and increased capacity utilization.
• The PLI scheme aims to make India a global manufacturing hub, reduce import dependence, and integrate into global supply chains.
• Key outcomes include the creation of new employment opportunities and enhancement of India's export competitiveness.
• Discussions are ongoing to potentially expand the PLI scheme to include new 'sunrise sectors' and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: PLI schemes offer incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
The scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing and attract foreign investment.
It covers multiple sectors like electronics, pharma, automobiles, textiles, etc.
Part of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives and outcomes of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in fostering domestic manufacturing and economic growth.
Discuss how the PLI scheme contributes to India's goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub and reducing import dependence.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities associated with expanding the PLI scheme to new and emerging sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: Factory assembly line with robotic arms, symbolizing modern manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: The 'Make in India' initiative was launched in 2014 to encourage companies to manufacture in India.

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Achieve Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Algorithm Development
GS3.explainer:
• A collaborative team of scientists from IIT Delhi and the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR) announces a significant advance in quantum algorithm design.
• The newly developed algorithm promises faster and more efficient solutions for complex computational problems, outperforming classical algorithms in specific scenarios.
• Potential applications span various fields, including drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, and advanced cryptography.
• This breakthrough highlights India's growing capabilities and expertise in the rapidly evolving field of quantum technology.
• The government's National Quantum Mission continues to provide crucial funding and infrastructure support for cutting-edge research.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement) to solve complex problems.
Qubits are the basic units of information in quantum computing.
National Quantum Mission was launched by the Indian government to promote quantum technology.
Applications include drug discovery, materials science, and cryptography.
mainsPoints: Discuss the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize various sectors.
Analyze India's efforts and achievements in quantum technology under the National Quantum Mission.
Examine the ethical and security implications of advancements in quantum computing, particularly concerning cryptography.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum bits (qubits) forming a network.
GS3.trivia: IBM launched the world's first integrated quantum computing system for commercial use in 2019.

GS3.headline: New Solar Power Project Inaugurated in Ladakh, Boosting Regional Energy Security
GS3.explainer:
• A large-scale solar power project is inaugurated in Ladakh, marking a significant step towards the region's energy independence.
• The project leverages Ladakh's high solar insolation to generate clean energy, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and grid power.
• It aims to provide stable and reliable electricity to remote areas, addressing long-standing energy deficits and developmental needs.
• The initiative is part of India's broader strategy to develop renewable energy infrastructure in border regions and promote sustainable development.
• Expected to foster local employment, reduce carbon footprint, and contribute to India's climate action goals.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Ladakh has high potential for solar energy due to its high altitude and clear skies.
Solar power projects contribute to India's renewable energy targets.
Such projects help reduce reliance on conventional energy sources in remote areas.
Part of India's commitment to climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing renewable energy infrastructure in regions like Ladakh.
Analyze the environmental and socio-economic benefits of large-scale solar projects in mountainous terrains.
Examine the challenges involved in implementing and maintaining renewable energy projects in remote, high-altitude areas.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels installed in a mountainous landscape of Ladakh under clear skies.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's third-largest producer of renewable energy.
